summary: The batch plan is approval-ready: it keeps the three V2 tickets as one coherent contract refinement, names the implementation order, carries the key data shapes, and explicitly blocks daemon/cloud/adapter overreach.

finding_log.summary: The plan names `codex cloud --help` as a version check, but implementation evidence must record unavailable-command output if the local CLI does not expose that subcommand.
evidence: Verification explicitly lists `codex cloud --help` and says not to submit tasks.
The batch is recipe-only, so command availability is evidence for wording, not a runtime dependency.
finding_log.next_action: During implementation, capture either the help output summary or the unavailable-command result in ticket evidence.

rubric_sections.name: spec-contract
findings: The three-ticket batch solves one coherent V2 problem: explicit invocation and external-runner compatibility without new scheduler ownership.
Non-goals are strong enough to prevent accidental daemon, listener, cloud wrapper, and external adapter work.
rubric_sections.next_action: Approve the batch as one implementation pass, preserving the order TASK-0121 -> TASK-0123 -> TASK-0122.

rubric_sections.name: implementation-plan
findings: The plan is file-map-first, names concrete seams, and gives a safe order that reduces wording drift across specs, conformance docs, and external recipes.
The strongest implementation guard is explicit: docs/tests/templates first, tiny Python seams only if they make conformance evidence real.
rubric_sections.next_action: Use the plan directly as the next build handoff after approval.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: Planning evidence links the inspected specs, tickets, code seams, and review references.
Implementation evidence is still future work by design, and the plan lists the exact commands and artifacts required for the build pass.
rubric_sections.next_action: When implementing, attach test outputs and recipe validation evidence to the ticket artifacts rather than relying on narrative summaries.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The plan respects the current code boundaries: `bin/codexter_invocation.py` remains diagnostic, `FileTicketAdapter` remains the only live adapter, and unsupported external compute stays blocked.
The main external dependency, Codex Cloud command vocabulary, is treated as documentation validation only and not a build-time side effect.
rubric_sections.next_action: Proceed to implementation after user approval; reject any build diff that adds polling, launchers, external clients, or auto-apply behavior.

next_action: Approve the V2 batch implementation and keep all three tickets gated until the next `$impl` pass moves them to building.
